title: Return structured failure navigation at the call site
goal: Make bounded Grabowski failures directly navigable by returning a stable machine-readable failure envelope with class, code, unchanged-retry safety, next state check and the narrowest permitted alternative route while preserving transport ambiguity and policy gates fail-closed.

acceptance.assertion: Applicable failures expose stable bounded fields including failure_class, error_code, retry_unchanged, next_action, alternative_tool or alternative_operation, authority_changed and evidence_ref when available.
The call-site taxonomy is mapped deterministically to the existing friction and execution-governor failure classes instead of creating an incompatible second classification system.

An alternative route is emitted only when it is no broader in authority than the failed call and its required preconditions are known; otherwise the field is null and next_action requests state verification or operator decision.

Mutation, connector-transport, filter and policy failures never imply safe unchanged retry unless target-state evidence proves that no effect occurred and the operation contract independently permits retry.
Existing human-readable errors remain understandable and legacy callers remain functional while typed callers can consume the new envelope.
Regression tests cover transport ambiguity, platform filter, policy gate, validation error, stale identity, missing target, role preflight failure and one successful narrower fallback recommendation.
A replay over representative friction cases records whether the envelope removes a separate diagnostic call without claiming that every external connector error can be structured server-side.
